question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted `idOrAliasOrEvmAddress` path parameter (e.g., an EVM address that resolves to a different account via `EntityService.getEncodedId()`) to retrieve NFT transaction records belonging to another account, causing unauthorized disclosure of transaction ownership data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user inject SQL metacharacters into the `idOrAliasOrEvmAddress` path parameter passed to `EntityService.getEncodedId()` to tamper with the underlying SQL query and alter which transaction records are returned?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply multiple `spender.id` query parameters with `eq` operators to `spenderIdInFilters` to force the `NftService.getSubQuery()` to build an unbounded `IN()` clause, causing the mirror node to return transaction records for all matching spenders and leaking cross-account approval data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `token.id` query parameter with both a range operator and an `eq` operator simultaneously to bypass `validateBoundsRange()` and cause the multi-union query to return transaction records outside the intended token range?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validateFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user submit exactly one `lte` and one `lt` spender.id filter to bypass the `spenderOperators.filter` count check (which only rejects >1 of the same type) and cause the SQL query to apply conflicting range conditions that expose unintended NFT transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user set `limit` to the maximum allowed value and combine it with a broad `token.id` range to cause the multi-union query (`lower`, `inner`, `upper`) to return an excessive number of transaction records, effectively enumerating all NFT transactions on the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted `idOrAliasOrEvmAddress` that resolves via `EntityService.getEncodedId()` to a valid account ID they do not own, and retrieve that account's staking reward transaction history, causing unauthorized disclosure of financial transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp filter with a `ne` operator to `extractStakingRewardsQuery()`, and since the `ne` check throws `InvalidArgumentError`, can they craft a boundary condition where the error message leaks internal SQL column names like `StakingRewardTransfer.CONSENSUS_TIMESTAMP`?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply multiple `timestamp` query parameters with `eq` operators to force `updateQueryFiltersWithInValues()` to build a large `IN()` clause, causing the staking reward query to return transaction records across many timestamps and bypass intended pagination limits?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the `startPosition` offset (hardcoded to 3) by supplying crafted filter combinations that cause `params.length` to misalign with positional SQL parameters `$1`/`$2`/`$3`, resulting in incorrect parameter binding that tampers with which transaction records are returned?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an account alias string to `EntityService.getEncodedId()` that matches multiple alive entities (triggering the `multipleAliasMatch` error path) to cause a denial-of-service on the rewards endpoint, preventing legitimate users from querying their transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted EVM address to `EntityService.getEncodedId()` that matches multiple alive entities (triggering `multipleEvmAddressMatch`) to cause a server error that leaks internal state about the transaction database?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getPaginationLink()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the `next` pagination link returned in the NFT response to inject crafted `token.id` or `serial.number` values that bypass `validateBounds()` checks and retrieve transaction records outside the originally authorized range?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the `next` pagination link (constructed via `utils.getPaginationLink()` using `lastRow.timestamp`) to supply a crafted timestamp that causes the rewards query to return transaction records from a different time window than intended?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `serial.number` filter without a corresponding `token.id` filter, and since `validateSecondaryBound()` throws an error, can the error message be used to enumerate valid filter key names and map the internal query structure for further exploitation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `token.id` with `gte` operator and a `serial.number` with `gte` operator to force the `lower` sub-query path in `getLowerFilters()`, causing the multi-union query to return NFT transaction records starting from an attacker-controlled lower bound?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply both `token.id=eq:X` and `token.id=gt:Y` simultaneously to trigger the `validateBoundsRange()` error, and use the resulting error response to confirm the existence of specific token IDs referenced in NFT transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `spender.id` with a `ne` operator to trigger `InvalidArgumentError` from `validateFilters()`, and use the error message content to confirm whether a specific spender ID has approved transactions on the target account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply the maximum number of repeated `spender.id=eq:X` parameters (up to `config.query.maxRepeatedQueryParameters`) to cause `spenderIdInFilters` to grow unboundedly and force the database to execute a massive `IN()` clause that leaks all NFT approval transactions for the target account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp range (`gte` + `lte`) that spans the entire epoch to `extractStakingRewardsQuery()`, bypassing the default `DESC` ordering limit and retrieving all staking reward transactions for any account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted account alias (via `AccountAlias.fromString()`) that resolves to a high-value account's encoded ID, allowing them to enumerate all NFT transactions associated with that account without any authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply duplicate `timestamp=eq:X` values to `updateConditionsAndParamsWithInValues()` causing `timestampInValues` to contain duplicates, which then get passed to `updateQueryFiltersWithInValues()` and generate a malformed `IN()` clause that alters which transaction records are returned?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `limit=0` or negative limit value that passes `buildAndValidateFilters()` validation but causes the multi-union query to return an unintended number of NFT transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `order=asc` to `extractStakingRewardsQuery()` to reverse the ordering of staking reward transactions, combined with a crafted `timestamp` lower bound, to enumerate transaction history from the beginning of an account's staking activity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `token.id` with `lte` operator and a `serial.number` with `lte` operator to force the `upper` sub-query path in `getUpperFilters()`, causing the multi-union query to return NFT transaction records up to an attacker-controlled upper bound?
